1: # Simple integration layer that lets Raix classes declare an MCP server
2: # with a single DSL call:
3: #
4: #   mcp "https://my-server.example.com/sse"
5: #
6: # The concern fetches the remote server's tool list (via JSON‑RPC 2.0
7: # `tools/list`) and exposes each remote tool as if it were an inline
8: # `function` declared with Raix::FunctionDispatch.  When the tool is
9: # invoked by the model, the generated instance method forwards the
10: # request to the remote server using `tools/call`, captures the result,
11: # and appends the appropriate messages to the transcript so that the
12: # conversation history stays consistent.
13: 
14: require "active_support/concern"
15: require "active_support/inflector"
16: require "securerandom"
17: require "uri"
18: 
19: module Raix
20:   # Model Context Protocol integration for Raix
21:   #
22:   # Allows declaring MCP servers with a simple DSL that automatically:
23:   # - Queries tools from the remote server
24:   # - Exposes each tool as a function callable by LLMs
25:   # - Handles transcript recording and response processing
26:   module MCP
27:     extend ActiveSupport::Concern
28: 
29:     # Error raised when there's a protocol-level error in MCP communication
30:     class ProtocolError < StandardError; end
31: 
32:     JSONRPC_VERSION = "2.0".freeze
33: 
34:     class_methods do
35:       # Declare an MCP server by URL, using the SSE transport.
36:       #
37:       #   sse_mcp "https://server.example.com/sse",
38:       #           headers: { "Authorization" => "Bearer <token>" },
39:       #           only: [:get_issue]
40:       #
41:       def sse_mcp(url, headers: {}, only: nil, except: nil)
42:         mcp(only:, except:, client: MCP::SseClient.new(url, headers:))
43:       end
44: 
45:       # Declare an MCP server by command line arguments, and environment variables  ,
46:       # using the stdio transport.
47:       #
48:       #   stdio_mcp "docker", "run", "-i", "--rm",
49:       #             "-e", "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN",
50:       #             "ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server",
51:       #             env: { GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "${input:github_token}" },
52:       #             only: [:github_search]
53:       #
54:       def stdio_mcp(*args, env: {}, only: nil, except: nil)
55:         mcp(only:, except:, client: MCP::StdioClient.new(*args, env))
56:       end
57: 
58:       # Declare an MCP server, using the given client.
59:       #
60:       #   mcp client: MCP::SseClient.new("https://server.example.com/sse")
61:       #
62:       # This will automatically:
63:       #   • query `tools/list` on the server
64:       #   • register each remote tool with FunctionDispatch so that the
65:       #     OpenAI / OpenRouter request body includes its JSON‑Schema
66:       #   • define an instance method for each tool that forwards the
67:       #     call to the server and appends the proper messages to the
68:       #     transcript.
69:       # NOTE TO SELF: NEVER MOCK SERVER RESPONSES! THIS MUST WORK WITH REAL SERVERS!
70:       def mcp(client:, only: nil, except: nil)
71:         @mcp_servers ||= {}
72: 
73:         return if @mcp_servers.key?(client.unique_key) # avoid duplicate definitions
74: 
75:         # Fetch tools
76:         tools = client.tools
77: 
78:         if tools.empty?
79:           # puts "[MCP DEBUG] No tools found from MCP server at #{url}"
80:           client.close
81:           return nil
82:         end
83: 
84:         # Apply filters
85:         filtered_tools = if only.present?
86:                            only_symbols = Array(only).map(&:to_sym)
87:                            tools.select { |tool| only_symbols.include?(tool.name.to_sym) }
88:                          elsif except.present?
89:                            except_symbols = Array(except).map(&:to_sym)
90:                            tools.reject { |tool| except_symbols.include?(tool.name.to_sym) }
91:                          else
92:                            tools
93:                          end
94: 
95:         # Ensure FunctionDispatch is included in the class
96:         include FunctionDispatch unless included_modules.include?(FunctionDispatch)
97:         # puts "[MCP DEBUG] FunctionDispatch included in #{name}"
98: 
99:         filtered_tools.each do |tool|
100:           remote_name = tool.name
101:           # TODO: Revisit later whether this much context is needed in the function name
102:           local_name = :"#{remote_name}_#{client.unique_key}"
103: 
104:           description = tool.description
105:           input_schema = tool.input_schema || {}
106: 
107:           # --- register with FunctionDispatch (adds to .functions)
108:           function(local_name, description, **{}) # placeholder parameters replaced next
109:           latest_definition = functions.last
110:           latest_definition[:parameters] = input_schema.deep_symbolize_keys || {}
111: 
112:           # Required by OpenAI
113:           latest_definition[:parameters][:properties] ||= {}
114: 
115:           # Store the schema for type coercion
116:           tool_schemas = @tool_schemas ||= {}
117:           tool_schemas[local_name] = input_schema
118: 
119:           # --- define an instance method that proxies to the server
120:           define_method(local_name) do |arguments, _cache|
121:             arguments ||= {}
122: 
123:             # Coerce argument types based on the input schema
124:             stored_schema = self.class.instance_variable_get(:@tool_schemas)&.dig(local_name)
125:             coerced_arguments = coerce_arguments(arguments, stored_schema)
126: 
127:             content_text = client.call_tool(remote_name, **coerced_arguments)
128:             call_id = SecureRandom.uuid
129: 
130:             # Mirror FunctionDispatch transcript behaviour
131:             transcript << [
132:               {
133:                 role: "assistant",
134:                 content: nil,
135:                 tool_calls: [
136:                   {
137:                     id: call_id,
138:                     type: "function",
139:                     function: {
140:                       name: local_name.to_s,
141:                       arguments: arguments.to_json
142:                     }
143:                   }
144:                 ]
145:               },
146:               {
147:                 role: "tool",
148:                 tool_call_id: call_id,
149:                 name: local_name.to_s,
150:                 content: content_text
151:               }
152:             ]
153: 
154:             # Return the content - ChatCompletion will automatically continue
155:             # the conversation after tool execution
156:             content_text
157:           end
158:         end
159: 
160:         # Store the URL, tools, and client for future use
161:         @mcp_servers[client.unique_key] = { tools: filtered_tools, client: }
162:       end
163:     end
164: 
165:     private
166: 
167:     # Coerce argument types based on the JSON schema
168:     def coerce_arguments(arguments, schema)
169:       return arguments unless schema.is_a?(Hash) && schema["properties"].is_a?(Hash)
170: 
171:       coerced = {}
172:       schema["properties"].each do |key, prop_schema|
173:         value = if arguments.key?(key)
174:                   arguments[key]
175:                 elsif arguments.key?(key.to_sym)
176:                   arguments[key.to_sym]
177:                 end
178:         next if value.nil?
179: 
180:         coerced[key] = coerce_value(value, prop_schema)
181:       end
182: 
183:       # Include any additional arguments not in the schema
184:       arguments.each do |key, value|
185:         key_str = key.to_s
186:         coerced[key_str] = value unless coerced.key?(key_str)
187:       end
188: 
189:       coerced.with_indifferent_access
190:     end
191: 
192:     # Coerce a single value based on its schema
193:     def coerce_value(value, schema)
194:       return value unless schema.is_a?(Hash)
195: 
196:       case schema["type"]
197:       when "number", "integer"
198:         if value.is_a?(String) && value.match?(/\A-?\d+(\.\d+)?\z/)
199:           schema["type"] == "integer" ? value.to_i : value.to_f
200:         else
201:           value
202:         end
203:       when "boolean"
204:         case value
205:         when "true", true then true
206:         when "false", false then false
207:         else value
208:         end
209:       when "array"
210:         array_value = begin
211:           value.is_a?(String) ? JSON.parse(value) : value
212:         rescue JSON::ParserError
213:           value
214:         end
215: 
216:         # If there's an items schema, coerce each element
217:         if array_value.is_a?(Array) && schema["items"]
218:           array_value.map { |item| coerce_value(item, schema["items"]) }
219:         else
220:           array_value
221:         end
222:       when "object"
223:         object_value = begin
224:           value.is_a?(String) ? JSON.parse(value) : value
225:         rescue JSON::ParserError
226:           value
227:         end
228: 
229:         # If there are properties defined, coerce them recursively
230:         if object_value.is_a?(Hash) && schema["properties"]
231:           coerced_object = {}
232:           schema["properties"].each do |prop_key, prop_schema|
233:             prop_value = object_value[prop_key] || object_value[prop_key.to_sym]
234:             coerced_object[prop_key] = coerce_value(prop_value, prop_schema) unless prop_value.nil?
235:           end
236: 
237:           # Include any additional properties not in the schema
238:           object_value.each do |obj_key, obj_value|
239:             obj_key_str = obj_key.to_s
240:             coerced_object[obj_key_str] = obj_value unless coerced_object.key?(obj_key_str)
241:           end
242: 
243:           coerced_object
244:         else
245:           object_value
246:         end
247:       else
248:         value
249:       end
250:     end
251:   end
252: end

1: require "json"
2: require "securerandom"
3: require "faraday"
4: require "uri"
5: require "digest"
6: 
7: module Raix
8:   module MCP
9:     # Client for communicating with MCP servers via Server-Sent Events (SSE).
10:     class SseClient
11:       PROTOCOL_VERSION = "2024-11-05".freeze
12:       CONNECTION_TIMEOUT = 10
13:       OPEN_TIMEOUT = 30
14: 
15:       # Creates a new client and establishes SSE connection to discover the JSON-RPC endpoint.
16:       #
17:       # @param url [String] the SSE endpoint URL
18:       def initialize(url, headers: {})
19:         @url = url
20:         @endpoint_url = nil
21:         @sse_thread = nil
22:         @event_queue = Thread::Queue.new
23:         @buffer = ""
24:         @closed = false
25:         @headers = headers
26: 
27:         # Start the SSE connection and discover endpoint
28:         establish_sse_connection
29:       end
30: 
31:       # Returns available tools from the server.
32:       def tools
33:         @tools ||= begin
34:           request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
35:           send_json_rpc(request_id, "tools/list", {})
36: 
37:           # Wait for response through SSE
38:           response = wait_for_response(request_id)
39:           response[:tools].map do |tool_json|
40:             Tool.from_json(tool_json)
41:           end
42:         end
43:       end
44: 
45:       # Executes a tool with given arguments.
46:       # Returns text content directly, or JSON-encoded data for other content types.
47:       def call_tool(name, **arguments)
48:         request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
49:         send_json_rpc(request_id, "tools/call", name:, arguments:)
50: 
51:         # Wait for response through SSE
52:         response = wait_for_response(request_id)
53:         content = response[:content]
54:         return "" if content.nil? || content.empty?
55: 
56:         # Handle different content formats
57:         first_item = content.first
58:         case first_item
59:         when Hash
60:           case first_item[:type]
61:           when "text"
62:             first_item[:text]
63:           when "image"
64:             # Return a structured response for images
65:             {
66:               type: "image",
67:               data: first_item[:data],
68:               mime_type: first_item[:mimeType] || "image/png"
69:             }.to_json
70:           else
71:             # For any other type, return the item as JSON
72:             first_item.to_json
73:           end
74:         else
75:           first_item.to_s
76:         end
77:       end
78: 
79:       # Closes the connection to the server.
80:       def close
81:         @closed = true
82:         @sse_thread&.kill
83:         @connection&.close
84:       end
85: 
86:       def unique_key
87:         parametrized_url = @url.parameterize.underscore.gsub("https_", "")
88:         Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(parametrized_url)[0..2]
89:       end
90: 
91:       private
92: 
93:       # Establishes and maintains the SSE connection
94:       def establish_sse_connection
95:         @sse_thread = Thread.new do
96:           headers = {
97:             "Accept" => "text/event-stream",
98:             "Cache-Control" => "no-cache",
99:             "Connection" => "keep-alive",
100:             "MCP-Version" => PROTOCOL_VERSION
101:           }.merge(@headers)
102: 
103:           @connection = Faraday.new(url: @url) do |faraday|
104:             faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
105:             faraday.options.open_timeout = OPEN_TIMEOUT
106:           end
107: 
108:           @connection.get do |req|
109:             req.headers = headers
110:             req.options.on_data = proc do |chunk, _size|
111:               next if @closed
112: 
113:               @buffer << chunk
114:               process_sse_buffer
115:             end
116:           end
117:         rescue StandardError => e
118:           # puts "[MCP DEBUG] SSE connection error: #{e.message}"
119:           @event_queue << { error: e }
120:         end
121: 
122:         # Wait for endpoint discovery
123:         loop do
124:           event = @event_queue.pop
125:           if event[:error]
126:             raise ProtocolError, "SSE connection failed: #{event[:error].message}"
127:           elsif event[:endpoint_url]
128:             @endpoint_url = event[:endpoint_url]
129:             break
130:           end
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Initialize the MCP session
134:         initialize_mcp_session
135:       end
136: 
137:       # Process SSE buffer for complete events
138:       def process_sse_buffer
139:         while (idx = @buffer.index("\n\n"))
140:           event_text = @buffer.slice!(0..(idx + 1))
141:           event_type, event_data = parse_sse_fields(event_text)
142: 
143:           case event_type
144:           when "endpoint"
145:             endpoint_url = build_absolute_url(@url, event_data)
146:             @event_queue << { endpoint_url: }
147:           when "message"
148:             handle_message_event(event_data)
149:           end
150:         end
151:       end
152: 
153:       # Handle SSE message events
154:       def handle_message_event(event_data)
155:         parsed = JSON.parse(event_data, symbolize_names: true)
156: 
157:         # Handle different message types
158:         case parsed
159:         when ->(p) { p[:method] == "initialize" && p.dig(:params, :endpoint_url) }
160:           # Legacy endpoint discovery
161:           endpoint_url = parsed.dig(:params, :endpoint_url)
162:           @event_queue << { endpoint_url: }
163:         when ->(p) { p[:id] && p[:result] }
164:           @event_queue << { id: parsed[:id], result: parsed[:result] }
165:         when ->(p) { p[:result] }
166:           @event_queue << { result: parsed[:result] }
167:         end
168:       rescue JSON::ParserError => e
169:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Error parsing message: #{e.message}"
170:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Message data: #{event_data}"
171:       end
172: 
173:       # Initialize the MCP session
174:       def initialize_mcp_session
175:         request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
176:         send_json_rpc(request_id, "initialize", {
177:                         protocolVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION,
178:                         capabilities: {
179:                           roots: { listChanged: true },
180:                           sampling: {}
181:                         },
182:                         clientInfo: {
183:                           name: "Raix",
184:                           version: Raix::VERSION
185:                         }
186:                       })
187: 
188:         # Wait for initialization response
189:         response = wait_for_response(request_id)
190: 
191:         # Send acknowledgment if needed
192:         return unless response.dig(:capabilities, :tools, :listChanged)
193: 
194:         send_notification("notifications/initialized", {})
195:       end
196: 
197:       # Send a JSON-RPC request
198:       def send_json_rpc(id, method, params)
199:         body = {
200:           jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION,
201:           id:,
202:           method:,
203:           params:
204:         }
205: 
206:         # Use a new connection for the POST request
207:         conn = Faraday.new(url: @endpoint_url) do |faraday|
208:           faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
209:         end
210: 
211:         conn.post do |req|
212:           req.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
213:           req.body = body.to_json
214:         end
215:       rescue StandardError => e
216:         raise ProtocolError, "Failed to send request: #{e.message}"
217:       end
218: 
219:       # Send a notification (no response expected)
220:       def send_notification(method, params)
221:         body = {
222:           jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION,
223:           method:,
224:           params:
225:         }
226: 
227:         conn = Faraday.new(url: @endpoint_url) do |faraday|
228:           faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
229:         end
230: 
231:         conn.post do |req|
232:           req.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
233:           req.body = body.to_json
234:         end
235:       rescue StandardError => e
236:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Error sending notification: #{e.message}"
237:       end
238: 
239:       # Wait for a response with a specific ID
240:       def wait_for_response(request_id)
241:         timeout = Time.now + CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
242: 
243:         loop do
244:           if Time.now > timeout
245:             raise ProtocolError, "Timeout waiting for response"
246:           end
247: 
248:           # Use non-blocking pop with timeout
249:           begin
250:             event = @event_queue.pop(true) # non_block = true
251:           rescue ThreadError
252:             # Queue is empty, wait a bit
253:             sleep 0.1
254:             next
255:           end
256: 
257:           if event[:error]
258:             raise ProtocolError, "SSE error: #{event[:error].message}"
259:           elsif event[:result] && (event[:id] == request_id || !event[:id])
260:             return event[:result]
261:           else
262:             @event_queue << event
263:             sleep 0.01
264:           end
265:         end
266:       end
267: 
268:       # Parses SSE event fields from raw text.
269:       def parse_sse_fields(event_text)
270:         event_type = "message"
271:         data_lines = []
272: 
273:         event_text.each_line do |line|
274:           case line
275:           when /^event:\s*(.+)$/
276:             event_type = Regexp.last_match(1).strip
277:           when /^data:\s*(.*)$/
278:             data_lines << Regexp.last_match(1)
279:           end
280:         end
281: 
282:         [event_type, data_lines.join("\n").strip]
283:       end
284: 
285:       # Builds an absolute URL for candidate relative to base.
286:       def build_absolute_url(base, candidate)
287:         uri = URI.parse(candidate)
288:         return candidate if uri.absolute?
289: 
290:         URI.join(base, candidate).to_s
291:       rescue URI::InvalidURIError
292:         candidate
293:       end
294:     end
295:   end
296: end

1: require "json"
2: require "securerandom"
3: require "digest"
4: 
5: module Raix
6:   module MCP
7:     # Client for communicating with MCP servers via stdio using JSON-RPC.
8:     class StdioClient
9:       # Creates a new client with a bidirectional pipe to the MCP server.
10:       def initialize(*args, env)
11:         @args = args
12:         @io = IO.popen(env, args, "w+")
13:       end
14: 
15:       # Returns available tools from the server.
16:       def tools
17:         result = call("tools/list")
18: 
19:         result["tools"].map do |tool_json|
20:           Tool.from_json(tool_json)
21:         end
22:       end
23: 
24:       # Executes a tool with given arguments.
25:       # Returns text content directly, or JSON-encoded data for other content types.
26:       def call_tool(name, **arguments)
27:         result = call("tools/call", name:, arguments:)
28:         content = result["content"]
29:         return "" if content.nil? || content.empty?
30: 
31:         # Handle different content formats
32:         first_item = content.first
33:         case first_item
34:         when Hash
35:           case first_item["type"]
36:           when "text"
37:             first_item["text"]
38:           when "image"
39:             # Return a structured response for images
40:             {
41:               type: "image",
42:               data: first_item["data"],
43:               mime_type: first_item["mimeType"] || "image/png"
44:             }.to_json
45:           else
46:             # For any other type, return the item as JSON
47:             first_item.to_json
48:           end
49:         else
50:           first_item.to_s
51:         end
52:       end
53: 
54:       # Closes the connection to the server.
55:       def close
56:         @io.close
57:       end
58: 
59:       def unique_key
60:         parametrized_args = @args.join(" ").parameterize.underscore
61:         Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(parametrized_args)[0..2]
62:       end
63: 
64:       private
65: 
66:       # Sends JSON-RPC request and returns the result.
67:       def call(method, **params)
68:         @io.puts({ id: SecureRandom.uuid, method:, params:, jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION }.to_json)
69:         @io.flush # Ensure data is immediately sent
70:         message = JSON.parse(@io.gets)
71:         if (error = message["error"])
72:           raise ProtocolError, error["message"]
73:         end
74: 
75:         message["result"]
76:       end
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "ostruct"
4: 
5: # This module provides a way to chain prompts and handle
6: # user responses in a serialized manner, with support for
7: # functions if the FunctionDispatch module is also included.
8: module Raix
9:   # The PromptDeclarations module provides a way to chain prompts and handle
10:   # user responses in a serialized manner, with support for
11:   # functions if the FunctionDispatch module is also included.
12:   module PromptDeclarations
13:     extend ActiveSupport::Concern
14: 
15:     module ClassMethods # rubocop:disable Style/Documentation
16:       # Adds a prompt to the list of prompts. At minimum, provide a `text` or `call` parameter.
17:       #
18:       # @param system [Proc] A lambda that generates the system message.
19:       # @param call [ChatCompletion] A callable class that includes ChatCompletion. Will be passed a context object when initialized.
20:       # @param text Accepts 1) a lambda that returns the prompt text, 2) a string, or 3) a symbol that references a method.
21:       # @param stream [Proc] A lambda stream handler
22:       # @param success [Proc] The block of code to execute when the prompt is answered.
23:       # @param params [Hash] Additional parameters for the completion API call
24:       # @param if [Proc] A lambda that determines if the prompt should be executed.
25:       def prompt(system: nil, call: nil, text: nil, stream: nil, success: nil, params: {}, if: nil, unless: nil, until: nil)
26:         name = Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(text.inspect)[0..7]
27:         prompts << OpenStruct.new({ name:, system:, call:, text:, stream:, success:, if:, unless:, until:, params: })
28: 
29:         define_method(name) do |response|
30:           return response if success.nil?
31:           return send(success, response) if success.is_a?(Symbol)
32: 
33:           instance_exec(response, &success)
34:         end
35:       end
36: 
37:       def prompts
38:         @prompts ||= []
39:       end
40:     end
41: 
42:     attr_reader :current_prompt, :last_response
43: 
44:     MAX_LOOP_COUNT = 5
45: 
46:     # Executes the chat completion process based on the class-level declared prompts.
47:     # The response to each prompt is added to the transcript automatically and returned.
48:     #
49:     # Raises an error if there are not enough prompts defined.
50:     #
51:     # Uses system prompt in following order of priority:
52:     #   - system lambda specified in the prompt declaration
53:     #   - system_prompt instance method if defined
54:     #   - system_prompt class-level declaration if defined
55:     #
56:     #  Prompts require a text lambda to be defined at minimum.
57:     #  TODO: shortcut syntax passes just a string prompt if no other options are needed.
58:     #
59:     # @raise [RuntimeError] If no prompts are defined.
60:     #
61:     # @param prompt [String] The prompt to use for the chat completion.
62:     # @param params [Hash] Parameters for the chat completion.
63:     # @param raw [Boolean] Whether to return the raw response.
64:     #
65:     # TODO: SHOULD NOT HAVE A DIFFERENT INTERFACE THAN PARENT
66:     def chat_completion(prompt = nil, params: {}, raw: false, openai: false)
67:       raise "No prompts defined" unless self.class.prompts.present?
68: 
69:       loop_count = 0
70: 
71:       current_prompts = self.class.prompts.clone
72: 
73:       while (@current_prompt = current_prompts.shift)
74:         next if @current_prompt.if.present? && !instance_exec(&@current_prompt.if)
75:         next if @current_prompt.unless.present? && instance_exec(&@current_prompt.unless)
76: 
77:         input = case current_prompt.text
78:                 when Proc
79:                   instance_exec(&current_prompt.text)
80:                 when String
81:                   current_prompt.text
82:                 when Symbol
83:                   send(current_prompt.text)
84:                 else
85:                   last_response.presence || prompt
86:                 end
87: 
88:         if current_prompt.call.present?
89:           current_prompt.call.new(self).call(input).tap do |response|
90:             if response.present?
91:               transcript << { assistant: response }
92:               @last_response = send(current_prompt.name, response)
93:             end
94:           end
95:         else
96:           __system_prompt = instance_exec(&current_prompt.system) if current_prompt.system.present? # rubocop:disable Lint/UnderscorePrefixedVariableName
97:           __system_prompt ||= system_prompt if respond_to?(:system_prompt)
98:           __system_prompt ||= self.class.system_prompt.presence
99:           transcript << { system: __system_prompt } if __system_prompt
100:           transcript << { user: instance_exec(&current_prompt.text) } # text is required
101: 
102:           params = current_prompt.params.merge(params)
103: 
104:           # set the stream if necessary
105:           self.stream = instance_exec(&current_prompt.stream) if current_prompt.stream.present?
106: 
107:           execute_ai_request(params:, raw:, openai:, transcript:, loop_count:)
108:         end
109: 
110:         next unless current_prompt.until.present? && !instance_exec(&current_prompt.until)
111: 
112:         if loop_count >= MAX_LOOP_COUNT
113:           warn "Max loop count reached in chat_completion. Forcing return."
114: 
115:           return last_response
116:         else
117:           current_prompts.unshift(@current_prompt) # put it back at the front
118:           loop_count += 1
119:         end
120:       end
121: 
122:       last_response
123:     end
124: 
125:     def execute_ai_request(params:, raw:, openai:, transcript:, loop_count:)
126:       chat_completion_from_superclass(params:, raw:, openai:).then do |response|
127:         transcript << { assistant: response }
128:         @last_response = send(current_prompt.name, response)
129:         self.stream = nil # clear it again so it's not used for the next prompt
130:       end
131:     rescue StandardError => e
132:       # Bubbles the error up the stack if no loops remain
133:       raise e if loop_count >= MAX_LOOP_COUNT
134: 
135:       sleep 1 # Wait before continuing
136:     end
137: 
138:     # Returns the model parameter of the current prompt or the default model.
139:     #
140:     # @return [Object] The model parameter of the current prompt or the default model.
141:     def model
142:       @current_prompt.params[:model] || super
143:     end
144: 
145:     # Returns the temperature parameter of the current prompt or the default temperature.
146:     #
147:     # @return [Float] The temperature parameter of the current prompt or the default temperature.
148:     def temperature
149:       @current_prompt.params[:temperature] || super
150:     end
151: 
152:     # Returns the max_tokens parameter of the current prompt or the default max_tokens.
153:     #
154:     # @return [Integer] The max_tokens parameter of the current prompt or the default max_tokens.
155:     def max_tokens
156:       @current_prompt.params[:max_tokens] || super
157:     end
158: 
159:     protected
160: 
161:     # workaround for super.chat_completion, which is not available in ruby
162:     def chat_completion_from_superclass(*, **kargs)
163:       method(:chat_completion).super_method.call(*, **kargs)
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Raix
4:   # The Configuration class holds the configuration options for the Raix gem.
5:   class Configuration
6:     def self.attr_accessor_with_fallback(method_name)
7:       define_method(method_name) do
8:         value = instance_variable_get("@#{method_name}")
9:         return value if value
10:         return unless fallback
11: 
12:         fallback.public_send(method_name)
13:       end
14:       define_method("#{method_name}=") do |value|
15:         instance_variable_set("@#{method_name}", value)
16:       end
17:     end
18: 
19:     # The temperature option determines the randomness of the generated text.
20:     # Higher values result in more random output.
21:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :temperature
22: 
23:     # The max_tokens option determines the maximum number of tokens to generate.
24:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :max_tokens
25: 
26:     # The max_completion_tokens option determines the maximum number of tokens to generate.
27:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :max_completion_tokens
28: 
29:     # The model option determines the model to use for text generation. This option
30:     # is normally set in each class that includes the ChatCompletion module.
31:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :model
32: 
33:     # DEPRECATED: Use ruby_llm_config.openrouter_api_key instead
34:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :openrouter_client
35: 
36:     # DEPRECATED: Use ruby_llm_config.openai_api_key instead
37:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :openai_client
38: 
39:     # The max_tool_calls option determines the maximum number of tool calls
40:     # before forcing a text response to prevent excessive function invocations.
41:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :max_tool_calls
42: 
43:     # Access to RubyLLM configuration
44:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :ruby_llm_config
45: 
46:     # A callable hook that runs before each chat completion request.
47:     # Receives a CompletionContext and can modify params and messages.
48:     # Use for: dynamic parameter resolution, logging, content filtering, PII redaction, etc.
49:     attr_accessor_with_fallback :before_completion
50: 
51:     DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 1000
52:     DEFAULT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS = 16_384
53:     DEFAULT_MODEL = "meta-llama/llama-3.3-8b-instruct:free"
54:     DEFAULT_MAX_TOOL_CALLS = 25
55: 
56:     # Initializes a new instance of the Configuration class with default values.
57:     #
58:     # Note: temperature is intentionally not defaulted. Setting a non-nil
59:     # temperature here would force it into every request payload, and some
60:     # providers (e.g. Anthropic's Claude 4.7 family on OpenRouter) do not list
61:     # `temperature` in their supported parameters. Combined with
62:     # `provider.require_parameters: true` (which Raix sets when `json: true`),
63:     # an injected default of 0.0 causes OpenRouter to reject the request with
64:     # "No endpoints found that can handle the requested parameters." Callers
65:     # who want a specific temperature should set one explicitly.
66:     def initialize(fallback: nil)
67:       self.max_completion_tokens = DEFAULT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS
68:       self.max_tokens = DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS
69:       self.model = DEFAULT_MODEL
70:       self.max_tool_calls = DEFAULT_MAX_TOOL_CALLS
71:       self.ruby_llm_config = RubyLLM.config
72:       self.fallback = fallback
73:     end
74: 
75:     def client?
76:       # Support legacy openrouter_client/openai_client or new RubyLLM config
77:       !!(openrouter_client || openai_client || ruby_llm_configured?)
78:     end
79: 
80:     def ruby_llm_configured?
81:       ruby_llm_config&.openai_api_key || ruby_llm_config&.openrouter_api_key ||
82:         ruby_llm_config&.anthropic_api_key || ruby_llm_config&.gemini_api_key
83:     end
84: 
85:     private
86: 
87:     attr_accessor :fallback
88: 
89:     def get_with_fallback(method)
90:       value = instance_variable_get("@#{method}")
91:       return value if value
92:       return unless fallback
93: 
94:       fallback.public_send(method)
95:     end
96:   end
97: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "active_support/core_ext/object/deep_dup"
4: require "active_support/core_ext/string/filters"
5: 
6: module Raix
7:   # Handles the formatting of responses for AI interactions.
8:   #
9:   # This class is responsible for converting input data into a JSON schema
10:   # that can be used to structure and validate AI responses. It supports
11:   # nested structures and arrays, ensuring that the output conforms to
12:   # the expected format for AI model interactions.
13:   #
14:   # @example
15:   #   input = { name: { type: "string" }, age: { type: "integer" } }
16:   #   format = ResponseFormat.new("PersonInfo", input)
17:   #   schema = format.to_schema
18:   #
19:   # @attr_reader [String] name The name of the response format
20:   # @attr_reader [Hash] input The input data to be formatted
21:   class ResponseFormat
22:     def initialize(name, input)

53:           response[:type] = "array"

55:           if input.size == 1 && input.first.is_a?(String)
56:             response[:items] = { type: input.first }
57:           else
58:             properties = {}
59:             input.each { |item| properties.merge!(decode(item)) }
60:             response[:items] = {
61:               type: "object",
62:               properties:,
63:               required: properties.keys.select { |key| properties[key].delete(:required) },
64:               additionalProperties: false
65:             }
66:           end
67:         when Hash
68:           input.each do |key, value|
69:             response[key] = if value.is_a?(Hash) && value.key?(:type)
70:                               value
71:                             else

73:                             end
74:           end
75:         else

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Regression coverage for the OpenAI-compatible response that
4: # `ruby_llm_request` returns. The non-streaming branch used to hand-build a
5: # hash with only `choices` and `usage`, dropping the upstream `id`, `model`,
6: # and `usage.prompt_tokens_details` that callers need to look up authoritative
7: # billing cost (e.g. via OpenRouter's /api/v1/generation) or to verify
8: # prompt-cache hits. See README of the consumer app: silent cost loss
9: # manifested as zero `cost_usd` on every distillation until this gap was
10: # closed.
11: class DummyMeaningOfLife
12:   include Raix::ChatCompletion
13: end
14: 
15: RSpec.describe Raix::ChatCompletion, "#ruby_llm_request response shape" do
16:   let(:instance) { DummyMeaningOfLife.new }
17: 
18:   let(:raw_response) do
19:     instance_double(
20:       "Faraday::Response",
21:       body: {
22:         "id" => "gen-2026-test-abc",
23:         "model" => "google/gemini-3-flash-preview",
24:         "provider" => "Google",
25:         "usage" => {
26:           "prompt_tokens" => 1234,
27:           "completion_tokens" => 56,
28:           "total_tokens" => 1290,
29:           "prompt_tokens_details" => { "cached_tokens" => 1024 }
30:         }
31:       }
32:     )
33:   end
34: 
35:   let(:fake_response_message) do
36:     instance_double(
37:       "RubyLLM::Message",
38:       content: "the answer is 42",
39:       tool_calls: nil,
40:       tool_call?: false,
41:       input_tokens: 1234,
42:       output_tokens: 56,
43:       model_id: "google/gemini-3-flash-preview",
44:       raw: raw_response
45:     )
46:   end
47: 
48:   let(:fake_chat) do
49:     instance_double(
50:       "RubyLLM::Chat",
51:       with_instructions: nil,
52:       add_message: nil,
53:       with_temperature: nil,
54:       with_params: nil,
55:       with_tool: nil,
56:       ask: fake_response_message,
57:       complete: fake_response_message
58:     )
59:   end
60: 
61:   before do
62:     allow(RubyLLM).to receive(:chat).and_return(fake_chat)
63:   end
64: 
65:   it "preserves the upstream generation id, model, and provider on the response" do
66:     response = instance.send(:ruby_llm_request, params: {}, model: "google/gemini-3-flash-preview", messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }])
67: 
68:     expect(response["id"]).to eq("gen-2026-test-abc")
69:     expect(response["model"]).to eq("google/gemini-3-flash-preview")
70:     expect(response["provider"]).to eq("Google")
71:   end
72: 
73:   it "merges upstream usage details (cached tokens) alongside the basic counts" do
74:     response = instance.send(:ruby_llm_request, params: {}, model: "google/gemini-3-flash-preview", messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }])
75: 
76:     expect(response.dig("usage", "prompt_tokens")).to eq(1234)
77:     expect(response.dig("usage", "completion_tokens")).to eq(56)
78:     expect(response.dig("usage", "total_tokens")).to eq(1290)
79:     expect(response.dig("usage", "prompt_tokens_details", "cached_tokens")).to eq(1024)
80:   end
81: 
82:   it "still returns the OpenAI-compatible choices array" do
83:     response = instance.send(:ruby_llm_request, params: {}, model: "google/gemini-3-flash-preview", messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }])
84: 
85:     expect(response.dig("choices", 0, "message", "content")).to eq("the answer is 42")
86:     expect(response.dig("choices", 0, "finish_reason")).to eq("stop")
87:   end
88: 
89:   it "tolerates a provider that does not expose a raw body (falls back to message model_id)" do

91:       "RubyLLM::Message",

101:     allow(fake_chat).to receive(:ask).and_return(barebones_message)
102: 
103:     response = instance.send(:ruby_llm_request, params: {}, model: "fallback-model", messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }])
104: 
105:     expect(response["id"]).to be_nil
106:     expect(response["model"]).to eq("fallback-model")
